title: Prefetch, Consumer Concurrency, Capacity, and Fairness · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective prefetch, concurrency, and capacity budget model; preserve message identity and timing, locate the first broken ownership boundary, and retain a deterministic regression case. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in prefetch, concurrency, and capacity budget model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Explain RabbitMQ per-consumer prefetch, deprecated global QoS, in-flight races, quorum caps, and the zero-means-unbounded hazard.
Bound concurrent handlers by CPU, memory, message bytes, downstream pools, deadlines, tenant fairness, and shutdown capacity.
Interpret consumer capacity with delivery rate, processing latency, ready and unacked depth, dependency saturation, and queue single-core limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Prefetch Window Semantics
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug messaging team handles case 1047 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine prefetch, active handlers, downstream capacity, and message-byte budgets; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Explain RabbitMQ per-consumer prefetch, deprecated global QoS, in-flight races, quorum caps, and the zero-means-unbounded hazard.

Inspect the Prefetch Window Semantics trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Prefetch Window Semantics.

Write the debug evidence note for Fair Tenant Dispatch: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug messaging team handles case 6095 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine prefetch, active handlers, downstream capacity, and message-byte budgets; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending.
